## 🌎 Geopolitics

### US Escalates Iran Campaign as Strait of Hormuz Warfare Intensifies—140 Targets Hit This Week

The Trump administration launched its third major strike wave against Iran on July 11–13, destroying approximately 140 Iranian military targets after Tehran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps attacked a Cyprus-flagged container ship in the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump declared the ceasefire "over" on July 12, and Iran has now shut the strait to all foreign shipping—a chokepoint through which roughly 20 percent of global crude oil passes. Natural gas prices have already spiked 40 percent; fertilizer prices tied to energy costs have jumped over 50 percent, signaling painful food-price shocks beginning immediately.

**Why it matters.** This is a direct macro shock hitting your portfolio and India's energy security simultaneously. The Hormuz blockade forces global energy markets into severe scarcity—shipping alerts are live, and traders are repricing geopolitical risk premiums across crude, LNG, and shipping futures. For your quant/prop work, watch the basis trades collapsing between WTI and Brent (regional disruptions create mispricings), track Indian rupee pressure as energy-import costs spike, and monitor how fast India's refineries pivot supply chains. The street is repricing energy logistics and regional inflation; the winners are positioned before consensus catches up.

**Action.** Watch CSIS and Al Jazeera's live coverage for flotilla routing changes and strike confirmations—shipping data from Lloyd's List and AIS feeds show real-time tanker diversions. Read the NPR deep-dive on US-Iran escalation mechanics and cross-check CNNMoney's commodity tracking against your models. The ICE natural gas contract expiry and the WTI/Brent spread are your leading indicators for how hard this hits Indian refining margins and monsoon-season food prices.

*Sources: [[https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/7/13/iran-war-live-us-bombs-iranian-cities-again-as-hormuz-standoff-intensifies](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/7/13/iran-war-live-us-bombs-iranian-cities-again-as-hormuz-standoff-intensifies)]([https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/7/13/iran-war-live-us-bombs-iranian-cities-again-as-hormuz-standoff-intensifies](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/liveblog/2026/7/13/iran-war-live-us-bombs-iranian-cities-again-as-hormuz-standoff-intensifies)) · [[https://www.csis.org/programs/latest-analysis-war-iran](https://www.csis.org/programs/latest-analysis-war-iran)]([https://www.csis.org/programs/latest-analysis-war-iran](https://www.csis.org/programs/latest-analysis-war-iran)) · [[https://www.npr.org/2026/07/11/g-s1-133212/us-iran-vessel-attack-strait-hormuz-gulf](https://www.npr.org/2026/07/11/g-s1-133212/us-iran-vessel-attack-strait-hormuz-gulf)]([https://www.npr.org/2026/07/11/g-s1-133212/us-iran-vessel-attack-strait-hormuz-gulf](https://www.npr.org/2026/07/11/g-s1-133212/us-iran-vessel-attack-strait-hormuz-gulf))*

---

### India Locks in Record Russian Oil Imports as Energy Diplomacy Hedges Against Hormuz Chaos

India imported 2.6 million barrels per day of Russian crude in June 2026—54 percent of its total 5 million barrel/day consumption and a historic high—while negotiating deeper BRICS energy partnerships under its 2026 chair. State buyers are now paying $15–20 per barrel discounts to Brent benchmarks, but the bigger play is India's push for BRICS-wide non-dollar payment mechanisms and exploration of Russian coking coal assets for steel-industry security. This represents India's permanent decoupling from Gulf dependency and explicit pivot toward Russia as strategic insurance against future Hormuz closures.

**Why it matters.** This is India's macro hedging in real time—and a template for how geoeconomic blocs are hardening around energy autonomy. For your consulting and quant work, the signal is clear: dollar-denominated energy markets are fragmenting into regional trade blocs. Watch Indian inflation dynamics closely: if Russian oil holds the import price floor, RBI has more room to hold rates, which affects rupee carry trades and India-US rate differentials. The BRICS currency talk signals central banks preparing for USD de-risking; that's a multi-year structural shift. Track Russian coal investment announcements and Indian steel OEM announcements—they're leading indicators of how fast supply-chain regionalization moves.

**Action.** Pull India's latest Oil and Gas Ministry statements and track Hindustan Petroleum and Indian Oil Corporation's crude-sourcing announcements month-by-month. Monitor RBI meeting notes for inflation commentary tied to energy costs, and watch India's interbank rupee forwards—USDINR basis spreads widen when geopolitical risk reshuffles supply chains. Read the BRICS grain piece on India's three-front energy diplomacy and cross-check Oilprice.com's India crude tracker for monthly import shift confirmation.

*Sources: [[https://www.indiaherald.com/Politics/Read/994893159/India-BRICS-2026-Energy-Diplomacy-Russia-Solar-Minerals](https://www.indiaherald.com/Politics/Read/994893159/India-BRICS-2026-Energy-Diplomacy-Russia-Solar-Minerals)]([https://www.indiaherald.com/Politics/Read/994893159/India-BRICS-2026-Energy-Diplomacy-Russia-Solar-Minerals](https://www.indiaherald.com/Politics/Read/994893159/India-BRICS-2026-Energy-Diplomacy-Russia-Solar-Minerals)) · [[https://oilprice.com/Energy/Crude-Oil/Indias-Russian-Oil-Boom-Outlives-the-Hormuz-Shock.html](https://oilprice.com/Energy/Crude-Oil/Indias-Russian-Oil-Boom-Outlives-the-Hormuz-Shock.html)]([https://oilprice.com/Energy/Crude-Oil/Indias-Russian-Oil-Boom-Outlives-the-Hormuz-Shock.html](https://oilprice.com/Energy/Crude-Oil/Indias-Russian-Oil-Boom-Outlives-the-Hormuz-Shock.html)) · [[https://infobrics.org/en/post/54925](https://infobrics.org/en/post/54925)]([https://infobrics.org/en/post/54925](https://infobrics.org/en/post/54925))*

## 🧠 Artificial Intelligence

### Grok 4.5 Forces Cost-Performance Reset: 4.2× Opus Efficiency at $2/$6 Pricing

SpaceXAI released Grok 4.5 on July 8, trained on real Cursor developer session data across tens of thousands of GB300s. The model clocks 80 tokens/sec output, solves the same tasks with 4.2× fewer tokens than Claude Opus 4.8, and costs $2 per million input tokens and $6 per million output tokens—roughly 60% cheaper on input, 76% cheaper on output. Cache hits drop to $0.50/M input (75% discount). Context window: 500k tokens.

**Why it matters.** This reshapes the inference economics for any production system. For long-horizon agentic tasks (multi-hour code synthesis, iterative debugging, complex reasoning loops), the combination of token efficiency + raw output speed + price creates a new cost baseline. Cursor's real-world developer data means the model learned on actual codebase patterns, not synthetic benchmarks. For teams running inference-bound workloads—chatbots at scale, enterprise automation, autonomous coding agents—this forces a hard re-evaluation of whether to stick with Opus or accept minor quality deltas for 2–3× cost savings and faster response latencies.

**Action.** Benchmark Grok 4.5 against Opus on your longest-running agentic workflows; cache overhead matters more on this model than on Opus, so profile token reuse carefully. If you're building Cursor-style long-context code editing, this is the model to test. For inference-bound deployments (sub-10ms latency requirements), the 80 tok/s throughput is competitive; measure end-to-end latency including cold cache. Consider a fallback strategy: Grok for repetitive/lower-risk tasks, Opus only for high-stakes reasoning or novel problem domains where the 1-2% quality gap matters.

*Sources: [[https://www.axios.com/2026/07/08/spacexai-grok-new-model]]([https://www.axios.com/2026/07/08/spacexai-grok-new-model]) · [[https://memeburn.com/grok-4-5-launches-with-budget-pricing-and-cursor-integration/]]([https://memeburn.com/grok-4-5-launches-with-budget-pricing-and-cursor-integration/]) · [[https://kingy.ai/blog/grok-4-5-benchmarks-pricing-context-window/]]([https://kingy.ai/blog/grok-4-5-benchmarks-pricing-context-window/])*

---

### Z.ai's GLM-5.2 Closes Frontier Gap as Open-Weights Rival: 1% Behind Opus on Long-Context Code

Z.ai (Zhipu AI) released GLM-5.2 in early July with a 1 million token context window, competitive frontier performance, and no regional restrictions. On long-running software engineering tasks lasting hours to days, it trails Claude Opus 4.8 by just 1% while outperforming GPT-5.5 and Opus 4.7 according to Z.ai's benchmarks. Pricing: $1.40 per million input tokens, $4.40 output—half the cost of Opus or GPT-5.5. Full source code is open.

**Why it matters.** This is the strongest Chinese model to reach parity with US frontier labs on narrow domains that matter for engineers (long-context code, vulnerability discovery, multi-step reasoning). For teams optimizing for cost per long task—RAG systems pulling 500k+ token contexts, multi-turn debugging sessions, code synthesis over sprawling codebases—the price and context window create a materially different deployment path. Open-source means zero vendor lock-in and the ability to run fully locally or on-premise, critical for regulated industries and organizations with data sovereignty requirements. Graphistry and Semgrep evals show comparable performance on cybersecurity tasks, broadening its appeal beyond pure coding.

**Action.** Run GLM-5.2 on your longest-context workflows (RAG retrieval chains, code review tasks with full repo context). Measure latency and token efficiency versus Opus; the cost-per-task math likely swings in GLM-5.2's favor for context-heavy work. If compliance or data residency force local deployment, GLM-5.2's open weights remove the infrastructure cost that normally makes local Opus prohibitive. Benchmark on your codebase-specific tasks (bug triage, security scanning); task-specific tuning may close the 1% Opus gap entirely.

*Sources: [[https://www.axios.com/2026/06/25/china-glm-52-open-source-hackers]]([https://www.axios.com/2026/06/25/china-glm-52-open-source-hackers]) · [[https://www.infoworld.com/article/4186136/z-ai-pitches-glm-5-2-for-long-running-software-engineering-tasks.html]]([https://www.infoworld.com/article/4186136/z-ai-pitches-glm-5-2-for-long-running-software-engineering-tasks.html]) · [[https://radii.co/article/z-ai-glm-5-2-disrupts-silicon-valley]]([https://radii.co/article/z-ai-glm-5-2-disrupts-silicon-valley])*

## 🇮🇳 India

### Cabinet Approves ₹10,000 Crore Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 to Fuel Venture Capital Mobilization

The Union Cabinet, chaired by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, approved the Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 with a ₹10,000 crore corpus to mobilise domestic venture capital across India's startup ecosystem. The new fund targets deep tech and technology-driven innovative manufacturing ventures requiring patient capital, with deployment expected to expand innovation beyond metro centres. Under the predecessor FoF 1.0, the entire ₹10,000 crore corpus was deployed across 145 Alternative Investment Funds, which invested over ₹25,500 crore in 1,370+ startups spanning AI, robotics, fintech, clean tech, space tech, and biotech.

**Why it matters.** For an AI engineer in Hyderabad building foundational models or applying to roles at deeptech startups, FoF 2.0 signals the government's sustained commitment to long-term capital allocation for patient, early-loss-making ventures. The explicit focus on deep tech and manufacturing-linked innovation means Series A and B-stage AI companies solving domestic use cases—language models, computer vision for agriculture, or industrial automation—now have a clearer funding pathway through government-backed AIFs. This reduces founder dependency on foreign capital and creates more locally-rooted investor networks.

**Action.** Track the NotifiedInvestments portal (likely updated on pib.gov.in or dpiit.gov.in) to identify which AIFs receive FoF 2.0 corpus. Cross-reference with your investment stage and sector to map which fund managers now have fresh deployment capital. For founders, outreach to AIFs already active in deeptech (prior FoF 1.0 data shows 145 AIFs; many will manage FoF 2.0 capital) should begin immediately.

*Sources: [[PM India Press Release](https://www.pmindia.gov.in/en/news_updates/cabinet-approves-startup-india-fund-of-funds-2-0-to-mobilize-venture-capital-for-indias-startup-ecosystem/)]([PM India Press Release](https://www.pmindia.gov.in/en/news_updates/cabinet-approves-startup-india-fund-of-funds-2-0-to-mobilize-venture-capital-for-indias-startup-ecosystem/)) · [[DD News](https://ddnews.gov.in/en/cabinet-approves-rs-10000-crore-startup-india-fund-of-funds-2-0-to-boost-venture-capital-ecosystem/)]([DD News](https://ddnews.gov.in/en/cabinet-approves-rs-10000-crore-startup-india-fund-of-funds-2-0-to-boost-venture-capital-ecosystem/)) · [[The CEO Magazine](https://startup.theceo.in/india-startup-india-fund-of-funds-2-0-growth-capital-efficiency-2/)]([The CEO Magazine](https://startup.theceo.in/india-startup-india-fund-of-funds-2-0-growth-capital-efficiency-2/))*

### India Clears Dixon-Vivo Joint Venture for 20-22 Million Annual Smartphone Manufacturing

India's government approved a 51/49 joint venture between Dixon Technologies and Vivo Mobile India on July 9, 2026, enabling the Chinese phone maker to manufacture smartphones domestically under long-standing investment screening rules for China-border nations. The JV will acquire Vivo's manufacturing assets and produce 20–22 million smartphones annually, with production commencing in December 2026 and ramping to 11 million units in FY27. The approval follows New Delhi's 2020 FDI rules requiring heightened scrutiny of investments from countries sharing land borders with India.

**Why it matters.** For a hardware engineer or electronics startup founder in India, this JV approval signals accelerating smartphone manufacturing ecosystem maturation beyond Apple's Foxconn partnerships. Dixon's entry as majority owner creates a blueprint for Indian companies to partner with global OEMs while maintaining domestic control and leverage. The 20+ million unit annual footprint will drive supply chain localisation—PCB suppliers, component integrators, testing labs, and logistics firms will cluster around Vivo/Dixon facilities, creating IP and manufacturing know-how jobs and contract manufacturing opportunities. For AI/IoT hardware startups, a mature, scale-ready ODM ecosystem in India reduces time-to-market for devices requiring local assembly.

**Action.** Identify Dixon's current manufacturing locations (typically Tamil Nadu, Telangana) to map supplier ecosystems nearby. Review Vivo's component sourcing reports (available via investor filings, supply chain audits, and industry databases like Crunchbase) to identify component gaps where Indian startups can bid for localisation. Track Dixon Technologies' quarterly earnings calls (BSE/NSE filings) for capacity expansion timelines and capex plans that telegraph upcoming supplier demand.

*Sources: [[TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/09/after-apple-indias-smartphone-manufacturing-boom-enters-new-phase-with-vivo-jv/)]([TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/07/09/after-apple-indias-smartphone-manufacturing-boom-enters-new-phase-with-vivo-jv/)) · [[The Tech Portal](https://thetechportal.com/2026/07/10/india-approves-dixon-vivo-jv-for-domestic-smartphone-manufacturing/)]([The Tech Portal](https://thetechportal.com/2026/07/10/india-approves-dixon-vivo-jv-for-domestic-smartphone-manufacturing/)) · [[Startup Talky](https://startuptalky.com/news/dixon-technologies-and-vivo-get-government-approval/)]([Startup Talky](https://startuptalky.com/news/dixon-technologies-and-vivo-get-government-approval/))*

## 📈 Markets

### India's inflation surprise triggers RBI tightening cycle expectations; rupee under pressure as imported costs surge

India's June CPI accelerated to 4.38% year-over-year, beating consensus of 4.20%, with broad-based pressures across food, fuel, and core inflation. The Nifty 50 closed at 24,211 (up 0.02%), Sensex at 77,616 (up 47 pts), and Bank Nifty at 58,131 (up 85.55 pts), showing controlled price action amid repricing of rate expectations. The India 10-year G-Sec yield jumped to 7.12%, reflecting forward guidance of imminent RBI hikes. Meanwhile, INR/USD edged to 95.67 as imported inflation surged to 8.13%—the highest in recent months—signaling sustained rupee weakness headwinds.

**Why it matters.** This inflation data catalyzes the RBI's policy pivot from accommodation to tightening, with experts pricing in rate hikes as early as August MPC meeting. SBI Research projects FY27 CPI averaging 5%, implying cumulative rate increases of 75–100 basis points through the fiscal year, taking repo to 6.0–6.25%. For quant/prop research, this is a structural macro shift: EM rate cycles are inverting against a backdrop of sticky US inflation (CPI still at 4.2% in May), compressing valuations for high-leverage equities and creating alpha opportunities in rates arbitrage and currency carry unwinds. Domestic flows into equities (Bank Nifty's resilience) mask underlying bond market turbulence as real yields contract.

**Action.** Watch the RBI's August 2026 policy decision for rate guidance—a 50bp hike is increasingly priced in. Monitor 10Y G-Sec yield direction; breach of 7.15% signals deeper rate adjustment expectations. Track rupee support levels near 95.80; further weakness implies imported inflation feedback loops tightening monetary constraints. Track Al-infra capex flows as real rates turn positive; near-term drag on valuations likely.

*Sources: [[India's Retail Inflation Accelerates to 4.38%, Raising Rate Hike Expectations](https://money.usnews.com/investing/news/articles/2026-07-13/indias-retail-inflation-accelerates-to-4-38-raising-rate-hike-expectations)]([India's Retail Inflation Accelerates to 4.38%, Raising Rate Hike Expectations](https://money.usnews.com/investing/news/articles/2026-07-13/indias-retail-inflation-accelerates-to-4-38-raising-rate-hike-expectations)) · [[Be ready for rate hike in coming months, warn experts](https://www.bizzbuzz.news/economy/be-ready-for-rate-hike-in-coming-months-warn-experts-1393368)]([Be ready for rate hike in coming months, warn experts](https://www.bizzbuzz.news/economy/be-ready-for-rate-hike-in-coming-months-warn-experts-1393368)) · [[India 10-Year Government Bond Yield](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/government-bond-yield)]([India 10-Year Government Bond Yield](https://tradingeconomics.com/india/government-bond-yield))*

---

### Geopolitical oil shock destabilizes tech equity complex; US equity selloff contrasts with Indian market resilience

US equities fell overnight on Middle East escalation as Trump announced a blockade on Iranian shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, sending Brent crude up to $78.59/barrel. The S&P 500 dropped 0.79% to 7,515.34, Nasdaq fell 1.55% to 25,873.18, and Dow declined 0.26% to 52,498.64, with semiconductor shares leading losses. The 10-year US Treasury yield edged up to 4.61%, reflecting persistent inflation expectations (May CPI at 4.2%) and a Fed holding pattern at 3.50–3.75%. Across the Atlantic, Indian markets diverged sharply: Nifty 50 and Sensex absorbed the geopolitical tremor with minimal downside, signaling regional liquidity differences.

**Why it matters.** The asymmetric equity correction—tech weakness offsetting energy strength in the US—exposes structural duration and sector beta mismatches in a sticky-inflation, higher-for-longer rates regime. For prop traders, this is a signal of regime fragmentation: commodity-linked capex (energy names, infrastructure plays in India) decouples from high-multiple software/services equities as geopolitical risk premia expand. India's relative stability on domestic fixed-income support and commodity-export tailwinds (gold, oil price levels favor emerging market FX hedges) creates tactical spread opportunities vs. US tech exposure.

**Action.** Watch oil curve term structure; if Brent sustains above $77, watch for yield curve steepening in UST (10–30Y). Monitor chipmaker stock breadth; a break below July 13 lows signals deeper tech repricing. Track gold as geopolitical hedge near ₹14,300/gram; breach higher likely triggers DXY weakness and cross-currency carry relief for INR. Calendar: Fed speakers this week may clarify inflation threshold; any hawkish repricing would compress India rate-swap spreads further.

*Sources: [[Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as chip stocks tumble, oil surges on US-Iran tensions](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-monday-july-13-dow-sp-nasdaq-113249278.html)]([Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq fall as chip stocks tumble, oil surges on US-Iran tensions](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/live/stock-market-today-monday-july-13-dow-sp-nasdaq-113249278.html)) · [[US 10 Year Treasury Note Yield](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/government-bond-yield)]([US 10 Year Treasury Note Yield](https://tradingeconomics.com/united-states/government-bond-yield)) · [[Brent crude oil - Price - Chart - Historical Data](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil)]([Brent crude oil - Price - Chart - Historical Data](https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil))*

## 🇮🇳 Indian Stocks

### TRENT (NSE): -12.4% on Q1 revenue growth miss despite 19% top-line expansion

Trent closed at ₹2,927.80 on July 7, 2026, down 12.44% from ₹3,343.80, as the fashion retailer's Q1 FY27 earnings disappointed despite solid absolute growth. Standalone revenue climbed 19% year-on-year to ₹5,666 crore but fell short of Street expectations for 22-23% growth. The rout pushed the stock to an intraday low of ₹2,931.15, with trading volume above average as retail investors took profits.

**Why it matters.** The sell-off signals a market recalibration: single-digit revenue deceleration at Trent—India's largest organized pure-play value fashion retailer—suggests consumer spending caution in discretionary segments, a read-across concern for all apparel and lifestyle names in the mid-cap space. Trent's 1,312 store count and continued expansion mask the real story: per-store productivity is falling and same-store sales are moderating as competition intensifies. For tech workers and urban professionals in Hyderabad or Bangalore, this flags slower discretionary consumption momentum in Tier 1 cities, validating broader market concerns about Q1-Q2 demand elasticity.

**Action.** Track Trent's Q1 earnings call transcript (posted on BSE/NSE announcements) for management commentary on store productivity trends and full-year guidance. Watch the ₹2,850 support level closely—a break below signals retail capitulation. Note: Noel Tata's announcement at the 74th AGM that July 2026 will be his last as Chairman before turning 70 in November adds leadership uncertainty; monitor for succession clarity in near-term disclosures.

*Sources: [[Trent Share Price Falls Over 11% on July 7, 2026](https://www.angelone.in/news/stocks/trent-share-price-falls-over-11-on-july-7-2026-here-s-why)]([Trent Share Price Falls Over 11% on July 7, 2026](https://www.angelone.in/news/stocks/trent-share-price-falls-over-11-on-july-7-2026-here-s-why)) · [[Trent shares crash 12% after Q1 update](https://upstox.com/news/market-news/stocks/trent-q1-update-revenue-rises-19-yo-y-to-5-666-crore-shares-in-focus/article-196469/)]([Trent shares crash 12% after Q1 update](https://upstox.com/news/market-news/stocks/trent-q1-update-revenue-rises-19-yo-y-to-5-666-crore-shares-in-focus/article-196469/)) · [[Trent Share Price fall 10% after Q1 Growth Revenue](https://www.indiainfoline.com/news/companies/trent-share-price-fall-10-after-q1-growth-revenue)]([Trent Share Price fall 10% after Q1 Growth Revenue](https://www.indiainfoline.com/news/companies/trent-share-price-fall-10-after-q1-growth-revenue))*

---

### HCL TECHNOLOGIES (NSE): +8.3% on Q1 profit beat, ₹12 interim dividend, $9.5B order book

HCL Technologies rallied 8.31% to ₹1,221.20 on July 13, 2026, as the IT services major posted a 20.35% year-on-year net profit jump to ₹4,626 crore for Q1 FY27, offsetting margin compression concerns. Consolidated revenue rose to ₹34,579 crore from ₹30,349 crore year-over-year, while the company announced an interim dividend of ₹12 per share (record date July 17, payable July 27). Volume eclipsed recent averages, signaling institutional accumulation.

**Why it matters.** HCL's earnings beat and robust $9.5 billion order book—including a marquee AI-led transformation deal with industrial giant SKF and a $2.6 billion annualized AI revenue run rate—validate the sector's pivot toward high-margin AI services. The 20% profit growth despite a cautious full-year guidance of 1-4% revenue growth suggests earnings accretion is decoupled from topline deceleration, a bullish signal for IT sector earnings seasons ahead. For tech professionals in India's software hubs, this reinforces that AI-driven transformation deals now command premium economics, even as vanilla managed services remain under pricing pressure.

**Action.** Review HCL's Q1 earnings call transcript (NSE filing, July 13) for specifics on deal duration, renewal rates, and AI as % of total contract value. The ₹12 interim dividend implies confidence in FY27 cash generation. Track the stock's hold above ₹1,200 support; a break lower could signal profit-taking ahead of rival Infosys earnings (scheduled July 23, 2026). Cross-check with TCS and Infosys playbooks to assess industry-wide AI penetration and pricing momentum.

*Sources: [[HCL Technologies Q1 Net Profit Jumps 20.35% YoY](https://www.freepressjournal.in/business/hcl-technologies-q1-net-profit-jumps-2035-yoy-to-4626-crore-interim-dividend-of-12-announced)]([HCL Technologies Q1 Net Profit Jumps 20.35% YoY](https://www.freepressjournal.in/business/hcl-technologies-q1-net-profit-jumps-2035-yoy-to-4626-crore-interim-dividend-of-12-announced)) · [[HCLTech posts strong Q1 with 20% profit rise](https://www.bizzbuzz.news/corporate/hcltech-posts-strong-q1-with-20-profit-rise-maintains-fy27-outlook-1397369)]([HCLTech posts strong Q1 with 20% profit rise](https://www.bizzbuzz.news/corporate/hcltech-posts-strong-q1-with-20-profit-rise-maintains-fy27-outlook-1397369)) · [[HCLTech Q1 FY27: Profit rises 20% YoY](https://www.businesstoday.in/markets/stocks/story/hcltech-q1-fy27-profit-rises-20-yoy-to-rs-4624-crore-announces-rs-12share-interim-dividend-542630-2026-07-13)]([HCLTech Q1 FY27: Profit rises 20% YoY](https://www.businesstoday.in/markets/stocks/story/hcltech-q1-fy27-profit-rises-20-yoy-to-rs-4624-crore-announces-rs-12share-interim-dividend-542630-2026-07-13))*

## 💹 Global Stocks

### TSM (NYSE): -2.5% to $423.43 as AI foundry strength battles broader chip sector weakness

Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing fell 2.5% to $423.43 on July 13, retreating from the previous close of $434.11, as the broader semiconductor complex capitulated on geopolitical and margin concerns. Yet TSMC's first-quarter results, released in the lead-up to trading, showed sales jumped 36% with June monthly revenue climbing 67.9% year over year, signaling sustained artificial intelligence infrastructure demand that contrasts sharply with memory chip weakness elsewhere.

**Why it matters.** TSMC's resilience despite sector selloff underscores that foundry capacity constraints for AI chips remain real. The company anchors the supply chain for NVIDIA, AMD, and other fabless designers; if TSMC's book-to-bill stays elevated through Q2 (results July 16 before U.S. open), it validates that capex cycles are lasting longer than current memory chip pessimism suggests. For India-focused semiconductor plays, TSMC's strength hints that the global AI infrastructure buildout, not a stalling capex super-cycle, is the base case.

**Action.** Watch TSMC's July 16 earnings call for management commentary on backlog depth and AI datacenter revenue mix. Key level: the $434 resistance; a close above $438 would recapture pre-selloff territory. 10-Q filing due within 60 days via SEC EDGAR.

### MU (NASDAQ): -5.5% to $XX.XX as memory stocks plunge into bear market; DRAM ETF down 20%+ since June 25

Micron Technology sank 5.5% on July 13 as memory chips entered a bear market, with the Roundhill Memory ETF (ticker: DRAM) down more than 20% from recent highs. SK Hynix and Samsung fell 6.0%-6.1% the same day, despite Samsung reporting operating profit of $59 billion and sales of $113 billion—figures that would ordinarily justify gains. The semiconductor sector as a whole shed $1.5 trillion in market value since June 25, with Micron alone losing roughly $350 billion of its value over that stretch.

**Why it matters.** The selloff reflects profit-taking and renewed doubt about margin durability. Memory is the most price-sensitive corner of semiconductors; the rout signals that investors are pricing in slower AI capex growth, higher commodity DRAM/NAND supply, or both. Unlike foundry (TSMC), where capacity is constrained, memory competes on cost, making any margin pressure immediately punished. The move is a recalibration risk for AI infrastructure plays globally—if datacenter customers are resisting memory pricing or deploying less DRAM per node, the capex super-cycle narrative weakens.

**Action.** Monitor next week's SK Hynix and Samsung full earnings for forward guidance on HBM and NAND pricing. Micron's next earnings (mid-September) will be critical; technical support sits at $66–$68 on the monthly chart. Filings available on SEC EDGAR (Micron 10-Q/10-K).

*Sources: [[finance.yahoo.com/memory-bear](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/article/micron-samsung-sk-hynix-just-dragged-memory-stocks-into-a-bear-market-154549356.html)]([finance.yahoo.com/memory-bear](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/article/micron-samsung-sk-hynix-just-dragged-memory-stocks-into-a-bear-market-154549356.html)) · [[timothysykes.com/MU-July-13](https://www.timothysykes.com/news/micron-technology-inc-mu-news-2026_07_13/)]([timothysykes.com/MU-July-13](https://www.timothysykes.com/news/micron-technology-inc-mu-news-2026_07_13/)) · [[247wallst.com/memory-selloff](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/07/micron-sandisk-and-western-digital-sink-7-as-samsung-earnings-spark-a-memory-selloff/)]([247wallst.com/memory-selloff](https://247wallst.com/investing/2026/07/07/micron-sandisk-and-western-digital-sink-7-as-samsung-earnings-spark-a-memory-selloff/))*

### Zoom Acquires Common Room to Inject Buyer Intelligence into Sales Platform

Zoom Communications entered a definitive agreement to acquire Common Room, an AI-native go-to-market intelligence platform, in early July 2026. The deal strengthens Zoom's Revenue Accelerator product line by embedding buyer intent signals and in-market account identification directly into sales workflows. Terms of the acquisition were not disclosed.

**Why it matters.** This signals consolidation in the AI sales tooling space where point solutions are being absorbed into larger platforms. Zoom's move suggests enterprise buyers prefer integrated stacks over tool sprawl, validating the thesis that post-CRM sales infrastructure needs AI-native design from the start. For founders in GTM tech, this underscores that standalone buyer intelligence plays may face headwinds unless they build defensible moats quickly.

**Action.** Track how Zoom integrates Common Room's capabilities into Zoom Revenue Accelerator's existing features. The real test is whether their combined product can compete with Salesforce's Einstein, Microsoft's Copilot for Sales, and dedicated platforms like Apollo. If you're building sales tooling, watch how this bundling strategy reshapes customer expectations around what "integrated AI" means.

### Mukesh Bansal's Nurix AI Acquires Verloop.io to Consolidate Voice and Chat Automation

Nurix AI, founded by Myntra co-founder Mukesh Bansal, acquired conversational AI company Verloop.io in July 2026. The acquisition combines Nurix's voice AI platform NuPlay with Verloop's chat automation engine, which processes over 20 million customer interactions monthly. Verloop founder Gaurav Singh joined Nurix's leadership team to lead product strategy and enterprise expansion. Financial terms were not disclosed.

**Why it matters.** This is a smart consolidation play in India's enterprise AI market where builders are racing to own full-stack conversational automation. The deal validates that the real value sits in customer traction and existing workflows—Verloop's 20M monthly interactions represent a fortress enterprise base. For Indian founders, Bansal's playbook shows that acquired revenue and customer relationships can be more valuable than pure technology in enterprise AI.

**Action.** Monitor how Nurix ports Verloop's banking and telecom customer relationships across voice and chat. The company claims backing from General Catalyst, Accel, and Prosus with prior $27.5M raised. If the integration succeeds, expect Nurix to raise Series B capital. This is a template for vertical consolidation in India's enterprise software market.

## 🩹 Health & Science

### Q32 Bio Achieves Durable Hair Regrowth in JAK-Inhibitor-Resistant Alopecia Areata

Q32 Bio reported 36-week results from the Phase 2a SIGNAL-AA trial of bempikibart, a selective lymphocyte kinase inhibitor, in 33 patients with severe or very severe alopecia areata. The trial met its primary endpoint with a mean 35.3% reduction in Severity and Extent Index (SALT) score. Notably, 40% of patients achieved SALT-20 and 44% reached SALT-30/SALT-50 endpoints, including one patient with complete hair regrowth despite prior JAK inhibitor treatment failure.

**Why it matters.** Alopecia areata affects 2% of the population, and many patients develop resistance to current JAK inhibitor therapies. Bempikibart's novel mechanism—rebalancing pathogenic T-cell activity rather than crude immunosuppression—offers a genuinely differentiated approach. The durable off-drug responses and favorable safety profile (zero Grade 3+ adverse events) suggest potential to expand the addressable population in a market estimated at $3+ billion for autoimmune hair loss treatments.

**Action.** Read the full trial readout when Q32 Bio publishes it; bempikibart advances toward a registration program in H1 2027. For clinicians, this validates investigating lymphocyte kinase inhibition in refractory autoimmune alopecia. For therapeutics builders, this is a blueprint for repurposing JAK-resistant patients into differentiated indications.

*Sources: [[Q32 Bio Press Release](https://www.streetinsider.com/PRNewswire/Q32+Bio+to+Report+36-Week+Topline+Results+from+Part+B+of+SIGNAL-AA+Clinical+Trial+of+Bempikibart+in+Alopecia+Areata+on+July+13,+2026/26757357.html)]([Q32 Bio Press Release](https://www.streetinsider.com/PRNewswire/Q32+Bio+to+Report+36-Week+Topline+Results+from+Part+B+of+SIGNAL-AA+Clinical+Trial+of+Bempikibart+in+Alopecia+Areata+on+July+13,+2026/26757357.html)) · [[RTTNews Coverage](https://www.rttnews.com/3666355/q32-bio-reports-positive-36-week-signal-aa-results-in-alopecia-areata-stock-up-in-pre-market.aspx)]([RTTNews Coverage](https://www.rttnews.com/3666355/q32-bio-reports-positive-36-week-signal-aa-results-in-alopecia-areata-stock-up-in-pre-market.aspx)) · [[Pharmaletter](https://www.thepharmaletter.com/biotech-news/q32-bio-reports-top-line-results-for-bempikibartin-alopecia-areata)]([Pharmaletter](https://www.thepharmaletter.com/biotech-news/q32-bio-reports-top-line-results-for-bempikibartin-alopecia-areata))*

### Vaccinex Finds Glial Biomarker Reversals With SEMA4D Blockade in Early Alzheimer's

Vaccinex presented biomarker data from the Phase 1b/2 SIGNAL-AD trial of pepinemab, a SEMA4D antagonist, at the Alzheimer's Association International Conference in London on July 13. The data showed that blocking SEMA4D signaling through astrocyte plexin-B1 receptors reverses glial activation signatures tied to neuroinflammation, supporting advancement to a Phase 2b expansion study (SIGNAL-AD2) in early symptomatic Alzheimer's disease.

**Why it matters.** Neuroinflammation driven by reactive astrocytes has emerged as a central driver of cognitive decline independent of amyloid-beta. Pepinemab's mechanism—damping astrocyte activation rather than clearing plaques—represents a distinct therapeutic angle in a crowded Alzheimer's pipeline. Reversible biomarker changes suggest the drug may interrupt disease progression at an earlier stage than amyloid-targeting approaches, opening commercial opportunity for combination regimens.

**Action.** Review the SIGNAL-AD poster session data when presented; the SIGNAL-AD2 Phase 2b design will likely inform trial endpoints around inflammatory cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) biomarkers and cognitive trajectories. For AI-in-health builders, this validates profiling astrocyte activation as a predictive biomarker for trial stratification and responder identification in neuroinflammatory conditions.

*Sources: [[BioPharm International](https://www.biopharminternational.com/view/vaccinex-to-present-novel-glial-biomarker-data-for-pepinemab-at-aaic-2026-outlining-path-to-phase-2b-in-alzheimer-s-disease)]([BioPharm International](https://www.biopharminternational.com/view/vaccinex-to-present-novel-glial-biomarker-data-for-pepinemab-at-aaic-2026-outlining-path-to-phase-2b-in-alzheimer-s-disease)) · [[BioSpace Press Release](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/vaccinex-to-report-new-biomarker-data-and-plans-for-phase-2b-clinical-trial-of-pepinemab-in-ad-at-the-alzheimers-association-international-conference-in-london-on-july-13-2026)]([BioSpace Press Release](https://www.biospace.com/press-releases/vaccinex-to-report-new-biomarker-data-and-plans-for-phase-2b-clinical-trial-of-pepinemab-in-ad-at-the-alzheimers-association-international-conference-in-london-on-july-13-2026)) · [[Quiver Quantitative](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Vaccinex+to+Present+New+Biomarker+Data+From+SIGNAL-AD+Study+Supporting+Expanded+Phase+2b+Trial+of+Pepinemab+in+Early+Alzheimer%E2%80%99s+Disease)]([Quiver Quantitative](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/Vaccinex+to+Present+New+Biomarker+Data+From+SIGNAL-AD+Study+Supporting+Expanded+Phase+2b+Trial+of+Pepinemab+in+Early+Alzheimer%E2%80%99s+Disease))*
